commentary_text: The model assigns Scotland a 45% win probability while the sportsbook consensus reaches 61%, a 16-percentage-point gap favoring Scotland at the market. This sharpening of confidence in Scotland's favour could reflect real-money flows incorporating recent form, injury news, or lineup changes that the model's last update did not capture. Haiti's win probability widens most under the model (25% vs 16%), suggesting the statistical baseline is less decisive than current information available to traders. Both sources agree Scotland is favored, but the market's sharper conviction signals either emerging confidence in Scotland's readiness or erosion of Haiti's prospects since the model was trained—details the dashboard cannot verify.
